title: ilex crenata subsp. radicans
intro: Ilex crenata subsp. radicans is a subspecies of holly native to Japan, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, spiny leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ilex crenata subsp. radicans include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, although specific preparation methods may vary depending on the intended use.
